units package

Submodules

units.admin module

Admin interface registration for the units app.

units.apps module

units.models module

class units.models.Unit(*args, **kwargs)

Bases: Model

Represents a military or organizational unit.

This model supports a hierarchical structure (tree) where units can have parent and child relationships (e.g., a Battalion belongs to a Brigade).

LEVEL_CHOICES = [('agency', 'Структура'), ('branch', 'Рід військ'), ('corps', 'Корпус'), ('brigade', 'Бригада'), ('battalion', 'Батальйон'), ('special', 'Спецпідрозділ')]

Choices for the organizational level of the unit.

name

Full name of the unit.

logo

Official logo or emblem of the unit.

short_description

Brief summary for previews (max 300 characters).

description

Detailed historical or operational description.

parent

Self-referential foreign key to create a hierarchy.

level

The category or depth level in the organizational tree.

children

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_reverse_many_to_one_manager() defined below.

fundraisingannouncement_set

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_reverse_many_to_one_manager() defined below.

id

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

units.tests module

units.urls module

URL configuration for the units application.

Defines routes for viewing individual unit details and their organizational data.

units.views module

units.views.unit(request, unit_id)

Display details of a specific unit and its hierarchical path.

This view fetches the unit by ID and reconstructs the full path from the top-level parent down to the current unit for navigation purposes.

Parameters:

  • request (HttpRequest) – The incoming request.

  • unit_id (int) – The primary key of the unit to display.

Returns:

Rendered unit detail page with hierarchy context.

Return type:

HttpResponse
